author: anon-13168fa2
content:
Delivered: Three files changed across all three critique points.

(1) Education framing softened:
- README Philosophy: 'bootcamps teach syntax AI generates / universities teach theory' → 'traditional education teaches theory and fundamentals, that knowledge is valuable and this program builds on it'
- README intro: 'you don't need a CS degree, those are cuneiform' → 'whether you're a beginner, student, or professional'
- New Literacy: 'You Are Not Learning to Code' → 'What This Path Is For', acknowledges CS/bootcamp/self-taught as valid starting points

(2) Beginner motivation strengthened:
- README: New 'What You'll Walk Away With' section listing tangible outcomes (working software, portfolio, badges, certificate, methodology)
- New Literacy: Leads with 'you have an idea you want to build, this teaches you how.' New 'What You'll Build' section listing concrete deliverables (bookmark manager, CLI tool, open source contributions, self-directed projects, capstone)

(3) Recognition system added:
- Portfolio Review: 5 phase completion badges (Foundation Set → Journeyman)
- Journeyman certificate on completion
- Reframed from 'we don't do certificates' to 'a certificate tells someone you completed a program, a portfolio shows them how, you have both'
